subject: Fwd: Fw: Need Assistance Detecting Unauthorized access of my accounts  (KMM10...
body:
This automatic response came back from Yahoo!  Did you register  yourself or 
did David register you on Yahoo.  I need to find out what  address you used 
and what secret question and answer you used to register or  they will not 
respond.  Please advise.
 
Darren K.  Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New  York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: _dkiesq@aol.com_ (mailto:dkiesq@aol.com) 

------------------------------  
IRS Circular 230 Legend: Any advice contained herein was not intended or  
written to be used, and cannot be used, for the purpose of avoiding U.S.  
federal, state, or local tax penalties. Unless otherwise specifically indicated  
above, you should assume that any statement in this email relating to any U.S.  
federal, state, or local tax matter was written in connection with the promotion 
 or marketing by other parties of the transaction(s) or matter(s) addressed 
in  this email. Each taxpayer should seek advice based on the taxpayer's 
particular  circumstances from an independent tax advisor.  
================================================================= 
Confidentiality  Notice: This communication and any attachments contain 
information from Darren  K. Indyke, Esq. that constitute attorney work product or 
that are otherwise  confidential and/or privileged. Except for personal use by 
the intended  recipient, or as expressly authorized by the sender, any person 
who receives  this information is prohibited from disclosing, copying, 
distributing, and/or  using it. If you have received this communication in error, 
please immediately  delete it and all copies, and promptly notify the sender at 
the above telephone  number or electronic mail address. Nothing in this 
communication is intended to  operate as an electronic signature under applicable  
law.
=================================================================



************************************** See what's new at http://www.aol.com


From: "J. Epstein" <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Subject: Fw: Need Assistance Detecting Unauthorized access of my accounts  (KMM109077396V60681L0KM)
Date: Thu, 15 Nov 2007 21:42:10 GMT
To: DKIESQ@aol.com

----- Forwarded Message ----
From: Yahoo! Account Security <account-security-help@cc.yahoo-inc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Thursday, November 15, 2007 4:04:48 PM
Subject: RE: Need Assistance Detecting Unauthorized access of my accounts (KMM109077396V60681L0KM)

Hello, 

Thank you for contacting Yahoo! Customer Care.

I appreciate the opportunity to address your report regarding Account 
Security. My goal is to provide you the best service possible, 
especially when it comes to safeguarding your account. I've sent you 
this auto-generated response as confirmation that my team has received 
your report.

So that we can assist you as quickly as possible, please reply to this 
email with the following information you supplied during your Yahoo! 
registration. If you do not remember what you supplied during 
registration, please provide us with the most recent information you 
updated to. 
Yahoo! keeps detailed records of the information you provided during the
registration of your account as well as any changes made to the account 
information.  We will be unable to provide login or other assistance 
without completely verifying your account.

Note: In order to ensure your email is sent to the correct department, 
please do not change the subject line. Doing so may delay your email 
from reaching a Customer Care representative.

1. Yahoo! ID 

2. Your name 

3. Date of birth (mm-dd-yyyy) 

4. Your alternate email address 

5. Secret Question and Answer 

6. Your city and state 

7. ZIP Code or Postal code you entered during registration 

8. Your country 


PROTECTING THE PRIVACY AND SECURITY OF YOUR ACCOUNT:
  http://security.yahoo.com/ 

COMPUTER VIRUSES:
  http://security.yahoo.com/malware.html 

GENERAL ACCOUNT SECURITY ISSUES:
  http://help.yahoo.com/help/us/security/index.html

SPOOF INFORMATION:
If you received an email impersonating Yahoo! and would like to report 
it please forward the email to mail-spoof@cc.yahoo-inc.com. If you have 
already been tricked into giving your password, please visit http://add.yahoo.com/fast/help/us/security/cgi_feedback
and supply as much detail as possible. 

For more information visit: 
  http://security.yahoo.com/password_scams.html 

Your email is important to us, and we strive to answer all reports 
within 12-48 hours.

Thank you again for contacting Yahoo! Account Security.

Regards,



Yahoo! Account Security Customer Care

For assistance with all Yahoo! services, please visit:

  http://help.yahoo.com/

New and Improved Yahoo! Mail - better than ever!



Original Message Follows:
------------------------

Dear Sirs:

My name is Jeffrey Epstein and I am the owner and authorized user of the
following two Yahoo! accounts: (1) jeeproject; and (2) 
littlestjeff.  My password for each of these accounts is as 
follows:
                              jeeproject - neptune          
                                littlestjeff - mermaid 

I am writing in reference to a telephone complaint  reference no. 
6262176  lodged on Thursday, October  25, 2007 with your premium care 
support line.  At that time I reported that I have reason to suspect 
that unauthorized third parties have been accessing one or both of my 
accounts and reading and distributing copies of my confidential e-mails.
I requested copies 
of all records of access, use and other activity with respect to these 
accounts over the past three months in order to discover the source of 
the unauthorized access during that three-month 
period.  Your premium care support representative advised me to e-mail 
my request to you at reportabuse@yahoo-inc.com.  
Please be advised that I have already changed my user passwords for both
e-mail accounts, so further changes in my passwords are not necessary at
this time.

I did as I was instructed and e-mailed to reportabuse@yahoo-inc.com my 
request for Yahoo!'s records of account access and activity for the 
above two accounts and I received the following form response from your 
reportabuse@yahoo-inc.com address:

"From: Yahoo! Mail <mail-abuse@cc.yahoo-inc.com>
To: jeffrey epstein <littlestjeff@yahoo.com>
Sent: Wednesday, October 31, 2007 4:46:38 PM
Subject: Re: (KMM59638111V63592L0KM)

Hello,

Thank you for writing to Yahoo! Mail.

Unfortunately, your message to us was missing the full Internet headers.
Without the full headers we will be unable to further investigate this 
matter. 

Email headers are used to deliver a message over the Internet and 
contain a record of the specific route that the message took. Full 
header information is included in every message that is sent. Depending 
on the setup of your email account and/or server, however, this header 
information may not be visible or otherwise available to you. Please 
consult with your email administrator or email program help information 
to determine the availability of this full header information.

At this time, we will need you to forward a copy of the message, as 
opposed to sending it as an attachment, because we are currently unable 
to accept attachments. Please include the following in your report of 
email abuse to assist us in a prompt and full evaluation:

1. Original subject line -- Please forward the email with a subject 
identical to the original subject.

2. Complete headers -- Email programs often display abbreviated headers.
To learn how to display the full headers in a Yahoo! Mail account, 
please visit the Yahoo! Mail Help Desk at:

http://help.yahoo.com/help/us/mail/config/config-11.html

For non-Yahoo! Mail users, please refer to the URL below for information
on how to get the full headers for your particular email client: 

http://www.haltabuse.org/help/headers/index.shtml 

3. Complete message body -- Please include the complete, unedited 
content of the email message in question. Please do not change or edit 
the message in any way.

If reports of email abuse are missing any one of these three items, we 
may not be able to further investigate such reports. We appreciate your 
efforts in reporting this abuse to Yahoo!.

I await your response so I may investigate your issue.

Thank you again for contacting Yahoo! Mail.

Regards,

Smith

Yahoo! Customer Care

41273099

For assistance with all Yahoo! services please visit:

http://help.yahoo.com/"


Obviously, Yahoo!'s response was completely inapplicable to my request. 
I was not writing to report an abusive e-mail.  So I again called your 
customer support line to request all Yahoo! records of account activity 
and access with respect to my Yahoo! accounts "jeeproject" and 
"littlestjeff" during the past three months.  The customer support line 
gave me the address to which I am e-mailing my request to you at this 
time.

Please advise me as to what I must do in order to obtain from Yahoo! all
records of account access and activity from August 1, 2007 through 
November 15, 2007 with respect to my two Yahoo! accounts "jeeproject" 
and "littlestjeff".

If this e-mail request is sufficient to obtain such records, please 
forward such records to me at 
my e-mail address littlestjeff@yahoo.com.  If additional information is 
required of me in order to process my request, please advise what 
additional information is required by e-mail to littlestjeff@yahoo.com. 
In any response you provide, I would appreciate it if you would also 
provide me with the name and telephone number of a representative with 
whom I may speak personally regarding this request.
Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Jeffrey E. Epstein
